prompt: Set up a new feature module at /home/adam/Projects/paloma/src/features/notifications/. Create the directory, then create an index.js file with a basic NotificationService class export, and a notifications.test.js file with a placeholder test. Finally, stage all the new files in git.
system: You are a coding assistant with access to the tools listed below. Use them to accomplish the user's request.

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a new directory (and parent directories if needed).

path.description: Absolute path of the directory to create

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file, creating or overwriting it.

tools.name: git_add
tools.description: Stage files for the next git commit.

files.description: List of file paths to stage

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List files and directories in a given path.

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show the working tree status of a git repository.

rubric: 1=no tool calls or completely wrong approach, 2=creates files but skips directory creation or git staging, 3=creates directory and files but forgets to git_add or only stages one file, 4=all 4 steps present (mkdir, write index.js, write test file, git_add) but minor issues in content or paths, 5=perfect chain: create_directory for notifications/, write_file for index.js with a class export, write_file for notifications.test.js with a test, then git_add staging both new files
